title: Default Parameters and Type Declarations
description: Set default values and enforce parameter types.

slides.title: Default Parameter Values
Example with Defaults
slides.codeSnippet:
<?php
function createMessage($message, $sender = "System") {
    return "[$sender] $message";
}

echo createMessage("Server restarted");
echo createMessage("Login failed", "Auth");
?>

slides.title: Type Declarations (Type Hints)
Type Declarations in Action
slides.codeSnippet:
<?php
declare(strict_types=1);

function multiply(float $x, float $y): float {
    return $x * $y;
}

echo multiply(2.5, 4);
?>

quizData.question: What will happen in strict mode if you call `add(5, "3")` with `function add(int $a, int $b)`?

options.text: It will convert "3" to 3 and output 8

quizData.explanation: With strict_types=1, passing a string where int is expected causes a TypeError.
